title: Rebuild vs NME
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jan 04, 2012 4:21 am Reichu wrote: But seriously, the T-shirts don't mean what you think they mean. The official title hasn't changed from when it was first announced years ago, and if you don't know (or refuse to believe) that the films are called ヱヴァンゲリヲン新劇場版 you will lose your footing in the Japanosphere VERY fast. The word "rebuild" gets bandied about in reference to the films, but it's not a project title in the slightest. It's more of a buzz word (bonus points for being in English) to indicate what they're doing, "rebuilding" Eva for the current generation. I'm guessing you can't read Japanese at all, so of course seeing "REBUILD" pops out at you, but please stop being so selective in what information you give credence.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 04, 2012 5:01 am Reichu wrote: The official title hasn't changed from when it was first announced years ago, and if you don't know (or refuse to believe) that the films are called ヱヴァンゲリヲン新劇場版 you will lose your footing in the Japanosphere VERY fast. The word "rebuild" gets bandied about in reference to the films, but it's not a project title in the slightest. It's more of a buzz word (bonus points for being in English) to indicate what they're doing, "rebuilding" Eva for the current generation.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 04, 2012 6:57 am Time for the change is 4 years too late. Changing titles at this point just means people will have a harder time finding what they're looking for. Click-throughs from search engines to these forums in the last month when they searched for at least the term "Rebuild": 114 Click-throughs from search engines to these forums in the last YEAR when they searched for at least the term "theatrical" or "movie" or "edition": 2 (zero for "edition"). Zero for "Gekijōban", "Gekijoban", "Gekijouban", or any Japanese characters in the last year. (Note: those are gathered by gostats, and don't include the custom search or site:forum.evageeks.org searches through google.) As for the rest of the internet: "Rebuild of Evangelion" returns almost 5 million results, "Evangelion New Movie Edition" only 161,000 and "Evangelion New Theatrical Edition" about 100,000.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jan 04, 2012 7:07 am ^ I was just gonna say, "The people who make the product don't get to decide what it's called. The official title is at best a suggestion."

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 04, 2012 7:14 am Monk Ed wrote: ^ I was just gonna say, "The people who make the product don't get to decide what it's called. The official title is at best a suggestion."

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 5:28 am Ornette wrote: As for the rest of the internet: "Rebuild of Evangelion" returns almost 5 million results, "Evangelion New Movie Edition" only 161,000 and "Evangelion New Theatrical Edition" about 100,000.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jan 05, 2012 6:35 am symbv wrote: So the problem is more widespread than I thought. It is in fact the whole English speaking world apparently. In Japan, where the movie was created and most of the fans resided, you do not get anywhere if you mention REBUILD, or even Rebuild Evangelion. And it has a proper and official English title. Isn't it a problem? Shall we perpetuate this misleading name just because the whole (English speaking) world is also wrong? We, who are the most dedicated and serious fans of Evangelion, out of all people?? I believe it is our duty to at least provide the proper information and show to everyone, particularly the new Eva fans, what the name of the new movie series actually is. If Rebuild is still too popular to be just taken away, why not call it "Evangelion New Movie Edition (aka Rebuild *unofficially* outside Japan)" ?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 8:15 am robersora wrote: Well, even the Wikipedia-page refers to it as Rebuild of Evangelion... http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rebuild_of_Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jan 05, 2012 8:40 am While I have to admit, that I failed reading the posts of Reichu and Ornette, I have read yours. Yet, I just can't see where the problem is if we stick with calling it rebuild. I mean, it doesn't change anything if you call it Rebuild, or New Movie Edition or just Re:Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 9:47 am robersora wrote: Well, even the Wikipedia-page refers to it as Rebuild of Evangelion... http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rebuild_of_Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jan 05, 2012 10:22 am Warren Peace wrote: I'll have to get one of those "Rebuild" T-shirts that Anno's staff wears so I can support the best title. Man, they sure are proud of that DVD feature. Enough to make T-shirts of it! Ahem.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jan 05, 2012 10:38 am Looks like you need some Wittgenstein in your life Symbv

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 11:30 am symbv wrote: So the problem is more widespread than I thought. It is in fact the whole English speaking world apparently. In Japan, where the movie was created and most of the fans resided, you do not get anywhere if you mention REBUILD, or even Rebuild Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 05, 2012 11:31 am We already had a thread about using "NME versus Rebuild" but it must have been locked since I can't find it anywhere. >_> As mentioned before, one of the first places the term "Rebuild of Evangelion" appeared was the Newtype Japan October 2006 issue (Kaworu and Rei on the cover) but "ヱヴァンゲリヲン新劇場版" was written above it. (Interestingly enough, Newtype USA December 2006 issue which featured the Japanese October (and some more recent stuff at the time) articles just say "Evangelion" for the cover.) "Rebuild of Evangelion" is mentioned in one source (can't recall where) as being a working title anyway, and sure enough when the DVDs/BDs were released, the title "Rebuild" was only used for the 30 minute Making Of extras ("Rebuild of Evangelion 1.01", "Rebuild of Evangelion 2.02") in the DVDs/BDs. Westerns do use the term since it's easier and already ingrained, but it is technically wrong. Funimation has never referred to the project as rebuild except for a DVD box tagline ("The rebuild of the original giant robot anime") and within the translated booklets in place of the word "remake". The 1.01 booklet translated by Funimation referred to the new movies as "New Theatrical Edition" where applicable. (Jo/1.0's Japanese title is translated as "Evangelion New Theatrical Edition: Prelude".) BUT, the 1.11 booklet in the same section (Introduction) referred to the film as "Evangelion:1.0 You Are (Not) Alone" instead. I thought it was interesting that Funimation decided to swap the translated Japanese title for the English/International one. Maybe they swapped one title for another to differentiate their 1.01 and 1.11 releases or something. (I can't remember what the translated 2.22 booklet referred to the new films as.) One last thing, an exchange with Carl Horn from last year pointed out the following: "I talked to two people I know at Funimation, and they said that they have never specifically been asked by the Japanese licensors to use the term "Rebuild" in reference to the new movies." (He also pointed out how it's interesting that GAINAX is officially in capital letters and khara is officially in lower case but that's neither here nor there.)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 11:47 am robersora wrote: While I have to admit, that I failed reading the posts of Reichu and Ornette, I have read yours. Yet, I just can't see where the problem is if we stick with calling it rebuild. I mean, it doesn't change anything if you call it Rebuild, or New Movie Edition or just Re:Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 11:54 am symbv wrote: I have zero experience of editing Wiki.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 12:04 pm Question: So you cannot just go and change it? I thought Wiki is free for all to edit? You need to pass a panel review now before you can edit? Is it because too many people edit wiki with ill intentions?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 12:12 pm symbv wrote: You know I wrote the post in reply to Ornette's post. If there is problem with the claim of the relative popularity of NME vs Rebuild, you may want to call Ornette's attention instead

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 12:17 pm Bagheera wrote: I'm calling your attention to it because you're claiming no one in Japan would know what you were talking about if you mentioned Rebuild of Evangelion, when over twelve million hits on Google Japan suggests otherwise. Ornette's claim is correct: the term has become ubiquitous both in the West and in Japan, and people familiar with the franchise at all in both places would get the reference either way.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 12:34 pm symbv wrote: Using English word in Google Japan proves nothing though. If you can compare how often it is used in Japanese websites, then it would be more meaningful.
